prevalence.trend: Stable to slightly increasing in urban areas due to pollution

incidence.source: SBPT and international estimates (e.g., GINA report adapted for Brazil)
incidence.trend: Increasing in children and urban populations

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (IHME) 2021 data extrapolated

vulnerablePopulations: Indigenous and rural communities with limited access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on GINA guidelines: Recurrent wheezing, breathlessness, chest tightness, cough; confirmed by spirometry showing reversible airflow obstruction (FEV1/FVC <0.7, >12% improvement post-bronchodilator)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: ~20-30% in primary care (via SUS, limited spirometry access)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~50% achieve good control with ICS/LABA therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows GINA/SBPT: Clinical history + spirometry; peak flow monitoring in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SUS protocol: Inhaled corticosteroids (ICS) as first-line; short-acting beta-agonists (SABA) for relief; stepwise approach per GINA; biologics (e.g., omalizumab) limited to severe cases in specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High urban pollution in São Paulo/Rio exacerbates symptoms; disparities in SUS access in North/Northeast; underdiagnosis in rural areas; medication shortages during economic fluctuations
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong pneumology societies (SBPT); nationwide SUS coverage for basic inhalers; public awareness campaigns; integration with telemedicine post-COVID for follow-up
